subject: Change LoggerFactoryWrapper to NetLoggerUtil
commit_message:
Change LoggerFactoryWrapper to NetLoggerUtil

Removed LoggerFactoryWrapper references to use NetLoggerUtil.

Issue-ID: POLICY-1499
Change-Id: I6f7066f4523736672e17d2e1e609c38c44852769
Signed-off-by: Daniel Cruz <dc443y@att.com>

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Jorge/Daniel - I'm concerned that this makes these model-impl very specific to Drools controller. We are about to move these model-impl into the policy/models repo so that they are accessible to all 3 PDP engines. Does these logging changes make that difficult?

Patch Set 3:

> Jorge/Daniel - I'm concerned that this makes these model-impl very
 > specific to Drools controller. We are about to move these
 > model-impl into the policy/models repo so that they are accessible
 > to all 3 PDP engines. Does these logging changes make that
 > difficult?

That's a good point Pam. I guess it depends on what the team is planning on doing in terms of logging conventions. The NetLoggerUtil allows 2 use cases, log() which abstracts the "LoggerFactory.getLogger()" call on the network logger and invokes before/after hooks of features for logging if any exist or getNetworkLogger() which returns the network logger itself. log() follows a drools specific kind of pattern of logging messages for the network log (Ex: [IN|DMAAP|TOPIC] <json-message>) but getNetworkLogger() allows you to dictate what the message will be since it is just a standard slf4j logger. 

My question would be is network log going to be used for all PDPs? If so then I think getNetworkLogger() is what we want to be calling here if policy/models is PDP agnostic and logging message formats may be different between PDPs. If the network log is going away then I think these reviews I have out should be put on hold to better fit the changes that are forthcoming.

Patch Set 3:

That's a good point, Pam.   I think it should be OK.  Here's my thought process on this:

One decision that it relates to the porting is what it is to be ported, if it is just the models with no networking, and may be that's how it should be, the *Manager.java classes that are touched here should be moved to somewhere else, maybe the "rest" module that is already there.

Regardless if we are moving the "rest" module within model-impl or not, the networking classes, ie. *Manager.java probably don't belong there, as they are not a model implementation, and should be moved.   That's something will have to be decided when porting.   In this regard, the "appc", "events" classes they are good as the model is kept separate from the implementation as they are placed in the dmaap bus.

The "network" logger is invoked right at the point the REST requests and only then, so if the "policy component" is only interested in the POJOS and not the networking functionality should be no impact.  

Another consideration is that the current components xacml-pdp, policy-api, policy-pap .. are using policy/commons: policy-endpoints for their dmaap needs, so in terms of dependencies there is nothing new added.

One more point, "network" logger is heavily used by developers and testers.   In integration calls, they just monitor this file to make sense of the flows.   It would be good to keep it consistent across components with same format, etc ..   We could even collect network logs across components and integrate them together if we need to understand what's going on in our policy subsystem, and they will have the same format.   Note that policy-api, policy-pap, drools-pdp, etc .. since they are using policy/common:policy-endpoints will have a network log with the same format printing the dmaap messages (done within policy-endpoints).

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

based on the above brain dump.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Jorge, I understand people like to look at the logs. They *could* be using the Dmaap notification topic. That's what its for. What we need is for people to accurately return back error information from either the dmaap messages or REST API's.

Liam, if you are ok we can bring this in and determine if there's needs to be a strategy for different PDPs regarding logging in the common models.

Per the first issue, I was thinking about this and maybe we clean up the API implementations and build an interface that they must inherit  from that supports the notification messages (with appropriate error message details for debugging) for Policy Notification Topic. That way we can set a requirement that way which would re-usable for each PDP engine.

Patch Set 3:

I agree with you Pam, the DMaaP notifications over POLICY-CL-MGT is the main source of analyzing what's going on in the system and provides a global view of the system.   It is very important to get those right and reporting accurately.  This is a complementary and secondary aid that I think still provides value as the granularity is on a per node basis, mainly for debugging.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1
